tests.name: Mixed verdict backlog (Approve + Return + ADR)
tests.description: Backlog of 12 stories under 3 epics. Architecture: GCP + Spring Boot + PostgreSQL + Kafka. Expected outcome: 8 approve, 2 return-to-product (ambiguous SLO language), 2 initiate-ADR (one needs Salesforce integration not in stack, one needs a new bounded context).
expected_outputs: Summary table totals: 12 reviewed, 8 approve, 2 return_to_product, 2 initiate_adr
Every gap row cites a UNIT/principle/ADR/NFR id from the architecture
INITIATE_ADR rows describe category-only (e.g., 'CRM API client') — never enumerate specific libraries
Per-story sections follow Templates 2 / 1a / 1b verbatim from references/output-templates.md

tests.name: Story-scope: single epic only
tests.description: Same fixture as EVAL-COMP-001 but story_scope='epic:E-PAYMENTS' restricts evaluation to the 4 stories under E-PAYMENTS. Expect 3 approve, 1 return-to-product, no initiate-ADR.
expected_outputs: Summary totals: 4 reviewed, 3 approve, 1 return_to_product, 0 initiate_adr
Stories outside E-PAYMENTS are absent from the COMPLIANCE-SPEC

tests.name: REPAIR: re-evaluate two stories after architecture update
tests.description: Existing COMPLIANCE-SPEC has US-15 = INITIATE_ADR (needed Salesforce). After ADR-007 was approved adding the Salesforce integration to architecture.units.integrations, the architecture path now includes it. failure_feedback targets US-15 and US-16 for re-evaluation. Expect: US-15 verdict changes to APPROVE (gap resolved by ADR), US-16 unchanged, all other stories preserved verbatim.

inputs.failure_feedback: Re-evaluate US-15 and US-16 against the updated architecture. ADR-007 has been approved and added the Salesforce integration. All other stories: preserve verdict and content.

expected_outputs: US-15 verdict changes from INITIATE_ADR to APPROVE; gap row removed; ADR-007 cited in the audit's CHANGE_LOG
All other stories: byte-equivalent to PREVIOUS_SPEC content for those sections
AUDIT records REPAIR_DIRECTIVES applied (US-15, US-16) and stories preserved (count)
